original_hypothesis.title: Last-Hour Voting Restriction Bypass via deposit_for Triggering Poke for Any Voted Token
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: VoterV3.vote() restricts voting in the last hour of an epoch (epochVoteEnd check) for non-whitelisted, non-AVM tokens. However, VoterV3.poke() lacks this restriction — it only checks epochVoteStart. VotingEscrow.deposit_for() has no authentication requirement and triggers poke() for any voted token. Since msg.sender for poke() is the VE contract (passes `msg.sender == _ve` check), anyone can force a vote weight recalculation during the restricted last hour by depositing 1 wei into any voted token.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The epochVoteEnd restriction was designed to prevent last-minute vote sniping. The deposit_for→poke path completely bypasses this restriction, allowing vote weight manipulation after the voting window closes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: poke() only checks `_timestamp <= epochVoteStart` (line 139), NOT epochVoteEnd; contrast vote() which checks `_timestamp >= epochVoteEnd` (line 161)

preconditions: Target token has active votes in the current epoch
Current time is within the last-hour restriction window (epochVoteEnd to epochNext)
Attacker has at least 1 wei of BLACK token

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: deposit_for(tokenId, 1) → voted[tokenId] is true → IVoter(voter).poke(tokenId) → poke calls _vote which recalculates weights
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin control over poke timing restrictions
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Last NO_VOTING_WINDOW (300 seconds) of each epoch

operational_questions: Can deposit_for with minimal value trigger meaningful weight changes?
What is the practical impact of recalculated weights in the last hour?
Does the timing align with AVM voting window?
validation_requirements: Confirm deposit_for(tokenId, 1) triggers poke during restricted window
Test weight recalculation magnitude with varying deposit sizes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker identifies high-value voted tokens during the epoch
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker accumulates BLACK tokens during the voting window
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: In the last hour, attacker calls deposit_for(targetTokenId, largeAmount) — tokens go into target's lock permanently
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Poke recalculates vote weights with inflated balance, shifting gauge emissions
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Capital required for meaningful weight change; tokens are locked permanently

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Last-minute gauge weight manipulation affects emission distribution
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No on-chain fix without upgrading VoterV3 or VotingEscrow

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: Minimal (1 wei) for trigger; substantial for meaningful impact
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Must be within epochVoteEnd to epochNext window (300 seconds with WEEK=1800)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: deposit_for calls during last-hour window followed by weight changes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Vote weight recalculations during restricted voting period
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Small deposits into others' tokens during last hour

validation_result.mechanism: Unauthorized vote weight recalculation via deposit_for
validation_result.impact_summary: Attackers can force a recalculation of any user's vote weight for 1 wei, applying time decay to reduce competing pools' weights right before the epoch ends.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The `VotingEscrow.deposit_for` function allows anyone to deposit tokens into an existing lock. If the token has already voted in the current epoch (`voted[_tokenId]` is true), it automatically calls `IVoter(voter).poke(_tokenId)`. In `VoterV3.sol`, the `poke` function checks if `msg.sender == _ve` (which is true when called from `VotingEscrow`), bypassing the `isApprovedOrOwner` check. Furthermore, `poke` does not enforce the `epochVoteEnd` restriction that is present in the `vote` function.

When `poke` calls `_vote`, it recalculates the user's vote weight using their current `balanceOfNFT(_tokenId)`. Because voting power decays over time, forcing a recalculation at the end of the epoch will apply the accumulated decay and reduce the target's vote weight.

An attacker can exploit this by calling `deposit_for(targetTokenId, 1)` on all veNFTs that voted for competing pools during the last hour of the epoch. This costs only 1 wei per target but reduces their vote weights, thereby increasing the relative weight of the attacker's chosen pools and redirecting gauge emissions.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof of signal accurately identifies that `poke` lacks the `epochVoteEnd` check. The trace from `deposit_for` to `poke` confirms that the `msg.sender == _ve` authorization allows any user to trigger this recalculation for any token.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The function lacks access control, allowing anyone to deposit 1 wei into any active lock. If the token has voted, it triggers `poke`.
The function allows `msg.sender == _ve` to bypass ownership checks. It also lacks the `epochVoteEnd` restriction found in `vote()`, allowing it to be called in the last hour of the epoch.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path:
1. Attacker calls `VotingEscrow.deposit_for(targetTokenId, 1)` during the last hour of the epoch.
2. `deposit_for` adds 1 wei to the lock and calls `IVoter(voter).poke(targetTokenId)`.
3. `VoterV3.poke` accepts the call because `msg.sender == _ve` and it is past `epochVoteStart`.
4. `poke` calls `_vote`, which reads the target's current `balanceOfNFT(_tokenId)`.
5. Due to time decay, the new balance is lower than the original vote weight. The target's vote weights are updated to this lower value, reducing emissions for their chosen pools.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is highly realistic and requires minimal capital (1 wei per target). By forcing a recalculation of competing votes right before the epoch ends, an attacker can apply the accumulated time decay to those votes, reducing their weight and increasing the relative share of emissions directed to the attacker's preferred pools.
validation_result.impact_assessment: High. Attackers can manipulate gauge emission distributions at negligible cost by forcing decay on competing voters right before the epoch ends. This undermines the fairness of the voting system and bypasses the intended last-hour voting restrictions.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the `IVoter(voter).poke(_tokenId)` call from `deposit_for`, or only allow it if `msg.sender` is approved or owner of the token.
Enforce the `epochVoteEnd` restriction in `poke` to prevent last-minute weight manipulations.
validation_result.replication_plan:
1. Create two veNFTs (A and B) and vote for different pools at the start of the epoch.
2. Fast forward to the last hour of the epoch.
3. From a third account, call `deposit_for(veNFT_A, 1)`.
4. Observe that veNFT_A's vote weight decreases due to decay, while veNFT_B's weight remains at its original value until the epoch ends.
